tasks.name: Fano Factor Control for CV Mechanism Validation
tasks.description: Verify CV is not purely a magnitude proxy. Compute Fano factor (CV^2/mean) per feature and test whether CV remains predictive of steering after controlling for activation magnitude. This tests the rate-distortion theory mechanism.

visualizations.description: CV vs steering effect colored by Fano factor
Regression results: CV and Fano factor predict steering

pilot.pass_criteria: CV remains significant predictor after Fano factor control (p < 0.01)

tasks.name: Non-Absorbed Baseline Comparison
tasks.description: Compare steering effects between absorbed high-CV features and non-absorbed features. Contextualizes absolute steering magnitude and determines if 'robust absorbed' is comparable to non-absorbed or still degraded.

visualizations.description: Steering effects: high-CV absorbed vs low-CV absorbed vs non-absorbed

pilot.pass_criteria: Absorbed high-CV steering effect > 50% of non-absorbed effect

tasks.name: Cross-Architecture Validation on Gemma-2-2B
tasks.description: Test whether CV-steering correlation generalizes across architectures. Use Gemma-2-2B JumpReLU SAE with architecture-specific median CV split. Replicates E1-E2 protocol on different architecture.

visualizations.description: Cross-architecture comparison: GPT-2 vs Gemma-2 steering effects

pilot.pass_criteria: CV effect replicates on Gemma-2 with p < 0.05

tasks.name: Held-Out Activation Patching Validation
tasks.description: Validate activation patching results on held-out words (not the 9 core words from pilot). Tests whether the 67.3% mean recovery is specific to the chosen words or a general property.

visualizations.description: Recovery rates: pilot core words vs held-out words

summary.validation_focus: CV-steering correlation mechanism validation and cross-architecture generalization
key_questions: Is CV predictive after controlling for magnitude (Fano factor)?
How do absorbed features compare to non-absorbed baseline?
Does CV effect generalize to Gemma-2 JumpReLU SAEs?
Are activation patching results generalizable beyond pilot words?
